name: Cornish Pasties
recipeInstructions:
Pastry:
Salt and pepper to taste Approximately 2 teaspoons cornstarch
PASTRY:
Combine flour and salt in a large bowl. Cut in lard until mixture resembles fine bread crumbs. Gradually add cold water, a little at a time, until the mixture binds into a soft dough. Place dough in a plastic bag or airtight container and refrigerate for at least an hour, or overnight. The leftover pastry can be frozen or used to make a pie while the oven is preheating.
FILLING:
Mix together half of the beef, half of the onion, and one potato for each filling. Season with salt and pepper and sprinkle with 1 teaspoon cornstarch. To assemble pasties, remove the dough from the refrigerator and prepare the filling. Preheat the oven to 425°F (220°C). Roll out the dough as you would for a pie crust and cut two large circles, about 10 inches in diameter. Place the filling in the center of each circle. Brush the edges of the pastry with water and fold one half over to meet the other, creating a half-moon shape. Press the edges lightly together. Fold the remaining edge back about half an inch and crimp firmly so it won't open during baking. Make two small slits near the top of each pasty and place them on parchment paper-lined baking sheets. Brush the pasties with milk and bake at 425°F (220°C) for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown. Reduce the temperature to 375°F (190°C) and continue baking for another 45 minutes.
